proverbs.text: If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together.
However long the night, the dawn will break.
It takes a village to raise a child.
Until the lion learns to write, every story will glorify the hunter.
When the music changes, so does the dance.
Even the best dancer on the stage must retire sometime.
Hold a true friend with both your hands.
He who is being carried does not realize how far the town is.
A bird that flies from the ground onto an anthill does not know that it is still on the ground.
Words are sweet, but they never take the place of food.
Where you sit when you are old shows where you stood in youth.
A wise person who knows proverbs reconciles difficulties.
A man who pays respect to the great paves the way for his own greatness.
When you follow in the path of your father, you learn to walk like him.
Wisdom is like a baobab tree; no one individual can embrace it.
When a king has good counselors, his reign is peaceful.
Cross the river in a crowd and the crocodile won’t eat you.
Do not call the forest that shelters you a jungle.
If you are filled with pride, then you will have no room for wisdom.
There is no medicine to cure hatred.
An army of sheep led by a lion can defeat an army of lions led by a sheep.
The hunter does not rub himself in oil and lie by the fire to sleep.
When the spider webs unite, they can tie up a lion.
He who learns, teaches.
The fool speaks, the wise man listens.
Where there is no shame, there is no honor.
A close friend can become a close enemy.
When elephants fight, it is the grass that suffers.
He who refuses to obey cannot command.
A leader who does not take advice is not a leader.
Every misfortune is a blessing.
Don’t set sail on someone else’s star.
Do not follow a person who is running away.
He who does not know one thing knows another.
Sticks in a bundle are unbreakable.
Two ants do not fail to pull one grasshopper.
Smooth seas do not make skillful sailors.
A roaring lion kills no game.
By trying often, the monkey learns to jump from the tree.
Do not look where you fell, but where you slipped.
Knowledge is better than riches.
Rain does not fall on one roof alone.
However long the moon disappears, someday it must shine again.
The death of an old man is like a burning library.
However long a log lies in the river, it will never become a crocodile.
Sleep is the cousin of death.
If the cockroach wants to rule over the chicken, then it must hire the fox as a body guard.
The eye never forgets what the heart has seen.
A tree is known by its fruit.
If you are building a house and a nail breaks, do you stop building, or do you change the nail?
A child is a child of everyone.
A small house will hold a hundred friends.
Pretend you are dead and you will see who really loves you.
The fire which warms us can also consume us.
Only a fool tests the depth of a river with both feet.
When the roots of a tree begin to decay, it spreads death to the branches.
What you help a child to love can be more important than what you help him to learn.
Patience can cook a stone.
When deeds speak, words are nothing.
He who has people is richer than he who has money.
However hot the soup, it must be eaten.
A river that forgets its source will dry up.
He who is destined for power does not have to fight for it.
Eyes that have seen the king do not bow to a chief.
A toad does not run in the daytime for nothing.
If a child washes his hands, he could eat with kings.
When a hand washes another, both become clean.
Where one thing stands, beside it another thing will stand.
When the moon is shining, the cripple becomes hungry for a walk.
A patient man cooks even a stone.
The strength of the crocodile is in the water.
Lies have many forms; truth has only one.
A man with too many friends has no friends.
It is the calm and silent water that drowns a man.
Two crocodiles in the same pond will quarrel.
If you are in hiding, do not light a fire.
The sound of the cooking pot does not announce who will eat.
When you are eating with the devil, you must use a long spoon.
When the legs cease to bend, life has been long.
Knowledge is a garden; if it is not cultivated, you cannot harvest it.
Haraka haraka haina baraka — hurry, hurry, has no blessing.
A small spark can burn a forest.
Slowly, slowly is the way to go far.
Where there are bees, there is honey.
He who has a head should not cover it with a calabash.
Visit your friend, but do not wear out the welcome.
There is no medicine for a fool.
A house without children is like a grave.
If you have one friend, you are blessed.
A man’s wealth may be superior to him; without wisdom he is poor.
The eye that has seen the sea cannot be satisfied with a pond.
A friend is someone you share the path with.
Umuntu ngumuntu ngabantu — a person is a person through other persons.
An ear that does not listen to advice will fall with the head.
Indlela ibuzwa kwabaphambili — the path is asked of those who have walked it.
An axe forgets, but a tree remembers.
Mabogo dinku a a thebana — many hands make light work.
A flea can trouble a lion more than a lion can trouble a flea.
Do not speak of a hyena’s child in front of its mother.
Where the heart is, the feet take you.
When the lion is sick, the antelope rests.
The teeth that laugh are the same that bite.
Dem na, fexe na — gone is gone.
A bird flies with two wings.
However far the stream flows, it never forgets its source.
Coffee and love taste best when hot.
Lend money to a bad debtor and he will hate you.
Three things drive a man from his house: smoke, dripping rain, and a scolding wife.
Donkeys quarrel until the lion arrives.
Truth is heavier than the mountain.
Wisdom does not come overnight.
The earth never tires of bearing a heavy load.
A guest is a king, but you don’t want him to stay forever.
An onion offered with love is better than a sheep offered with hatred.
Patience is the key to relief.
When the hen wants to lay an egg, no one stops her.
When you give, you teach how to give.
A child does not see the danger in the bush.
What a child says, he has heard at home.
He who has been bitten by a snake fears a lizard.
A small axe can cut down a big tree.
A good name is better than gold.
Anger has no eyes.
The chameleon changes colour to match the earth; the earth does not change to match the chameleon.
A child can play with its mother’s breasts but not with its father’s beard.
Wherever a man goes to dwell, his character goes with him.
If you offend, ask for pardon; if offended, forgive.
He who eats alone cannot discuss the taste of the food with others.
What an old man can see while sitting, a young man cannot see standing.
A man who fears suffering is already suffering from what he fears.
The cock that crows in the morning belongs to one household, but its voice is the property of the neighbourhood.
When the cock is drunk, he forgets about the hawk.
By the time the fool has learned the game, the players have dispersed.
One falsehood spoils a thousand truths.
Asiyesikia la mkuu huvunjika guu — he who does not heed the elder breaks his leg.
Mwanzo ngumu, mwisho mtamu — hard beginning, sweet end.
Mtaka cha mvunguni sharti ainame — he who wants what is under the bed must bend down.
Akili ni mali — wisdom is wealth.
Penye nia, pana njia — where there is a will, there is a way.
Cattle are born with ears; their horns grow later.
Inyoka emnyama ngeyangaphakathi — the dark snake is the one within.
A leopard cannot be caught with words.
Lentswe la kgosi le agelwa lesaka — the chief’s word builds a kraal.
Chinokanganwa idemo, chitsiga hachikanganwe — the axe forgets, the tree does not.
Nit nitay garabam — the human being is the medicine of the human being.
The crocodile and the fish drink from the same water; only the fish smiles.
Two thieves do not steal from each other.
Wisdom is wealth that is not stolen.
Kindness begets kindness.
He who climbs the mountain knows how heavy the wind is.
The dogs bark, but the caravan moves on.
Take what is light and avoid what is heavy.
When the river makes a bend, it is to feed the village.
If you kick a stone in anger, you will hurt your own foot.
The river that does not know its own banks will overflow.
If you want to know the end, look at the beginning.
